name: matthorrigan.com house style
summary: Canonical design tokens for matthorrigan.com. Machine-readable mirror of brand/house-style.md. Build tools (the CV builder, a dossier builder, future tools) should read this and/or import brand/brand.css so every page shares one look.

color._note: Light 'document' context — used by the index, CV, and all tools. The game splash (autofac.html) is a separate dark palette; see `gamePalette`.

highlight._use: THE single interaction/highlight colour: menubar hover & current item, key-word highlights, text selection, focus rings. Appears in code as --mh-blue (menubar), --favcolor (index), --focus / --flare-cool (tools).

accent._use: Document accent for the CV and tools: links, primary buttons, labels, progress fills. A deep violet chosen to sit as the third house colour alongside the cyan highlight and the orange flare. NOT used in the menubar (the menubar is cyan-only). Code: --accent / --accent-strong / --accent-wash / --on-accent.

flare._use: The orange house colour. `warm`/`cool` are the soft orange->cyan 'cloud' backdrop on home/landing screens (code: --secondcolor on index; --flare-warm / --flare-cool on tools). `strong` (deep burnt orange) + `wash` (light orange) make an orange callout that keeps a light-bg/dark-text contrast, e.g. ExamTimer's exam-rules warning. Code: --flare-strong / --flare-wash.

status._use: Kept deliberately distinct from the violet brand accent so errors/warnings read as errors (red vs violet). `dangerStrong` is the hover/active (ExamTimer's danger button); `ok` is only used by ExamTimer.

leaf._description: The signature 'leaf' corner — the brand's defining shape. An asymmetric corner: a gentle elliptical SWEEP at the TOP-LEFT and BOTTOM-RIGHT corners, near-square at the TOP-RIGHT and BOTTOM-LEFT.
leaf.orientation: big sweep at top-left + bottom-right; near-square at top-right + bottom-left — never flip it
leaf.syntax: CSS border-radius longhand: 'h-TL h-TR h-BR h-BL / v-TL v-TR v-BR v-BL'
leaf.rule: Use FIXED elliptical radii (px), NOT percentages, so the corner arc is identical on a narrow pill and a wide panel. (Percentages stretch the corner flat as elements widen.) The one exception is fluid/inline text — see `fluidPercent`.

radiiUse.card: cards, panels, modals, drop zones, sections (code: --leaf-lg)
radiiUse.panel: the menubar dropdown panel — same shape as `card`, slightly shallower (code: --mh-leaf-box)
radiiUse.barBottomRight: applied as `border-bottom-right-radius` to the fixed menubar so its bottom-right corner sweeps (code: --mh-leaf-bar)
radiiUse.fluidPercent: fluid/inline elements that must scale with content: the index .blacklight key-word highlight and ::selection feel. The hero photo uses a sibling fluid value 25% 0% 50% 0%.

neverLeaf: progress-bar tracks & fills — keep border-radius: 999px (rounded ends read correctly on a thin bar)
true circles (avatars, numbered badges, dots) — keep border-radius: 50%
surfaces intentionally squared for print or fullscreen/projection — keep border-radius: 0

highlight._description: The cyan 'highlighter' treatment — the brand's interaction feedback.
highlight.appliesTo: menubar hover & current item; the CV menubar buttons (Web / Print / PDF / Download PDF ↓); .blacklight key-word highlights on the index

selection._note: ::selection ignores border-radius and filter, so only background + colour apply. Define site-wide for a consistent selection colour.

externalLinkCue._description: Curved underline on menubar links that open ANOTHER website (different origin). A mostly-flat line that sweeps up at the bottom-right, echoing the leaf's bottom edge. Implemented as an SVG mask so it follows the text width.
externalLinkCue.appliesWhen: the link's resolved origin differs from the page's origin (works on the live domain and in local file:// previews)

type.bodySerif: "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Palatino, Georgia, serif

usage.sans: UI, headings, the menubar, CV headings, AND the tool app bodies. Code: --sans-font.
usage.bodySerif: Long-form prose — the CV body. Available to tools for prose blocks, but the tools (UI-dense apps) set their <body> to --sans-font. Code: --body-font.
usage.mono: Code / tabular previews (e.g. MCQer's format reference). Code: --mono-font, defined where used.
usage.menubar: pinned to a system-sans stack at a FIXED 13px / weight 600 (px, not rem) so the bar is pixel-identical across pages with different root font-sizes. menubar.js hard-codes this stack (and additionally lists `system-ui`) rather than referencing --sans-font, on purpose, so the bar is self-contained.
usage.indexException: The home page (index.html) is bespoke: Georgia serif body with Tahoma/Arial small-caps headings. New tools should prefer --sans-font (UI) + --body-font (prose) instead.

shadow._use: card = main elevated surfaces (tools); bar = the fixed menubar; dropdown = the menubar panel

focus._use: Apply the cyan ring on :focus-visible. Code: var(--focus) = the cyan.

callout._description: Left-accent info/notice strip — an intentional NON-leaf exception.
callout.borderLeft: 4px solid var(--accent) — or var(--danger) for errors

layout._use: Shared max-width for a tool's main card/panel (code: --card-max), so MCQer / SeatPlanner / ExamTimer read as one set. Chosen for comfortable reading in the single-column tool (MCQer) rather than only the grid tools; wide content like Seat Planner's chart scrolls inside its own pane instead of stretching the card.

legacy._note: All three tools define --radius: 8px. It predates the leaf system and is no longer used for corners (those use --leaf*); kept as a spacing/scalar token.

menubar._description: One shared, framework-free menubar for the whole site.
menubar.editLinksIn: the GAMES / TOOLS / LINKS_BEFORE_DROPDOWNS / LINKS_AFTER_DROPDOWNS arrays at the top of menubar.js
menubar.modes: Injects a fixed #mh-menubar bar on a normal page; on the CV it splices the nav into the existing #cv-menubar instead.

bar.boxSizing: border-box (enforced by the bar so a host page's box model can't resize it)

consume._howToUse: New tools should: (1) <link> brand/brand.css for the tokens + utility classes; (2) include menubar.js for the shared nav; (3) reference radii via var(--leaf*, <fallback>) so they render even before menubar.js runs; (4) keep the menubar cyan-only and the document body on the violet accent; (5) cap the main card at var(--card-max) so the tools read as one set.
consume._status: The existing reference pages each INLINE their own :root token block (they predate brand.css); brand.css/tokens.json are the canonical source for NEW tools and carry identical values. The CV defines only a subset (--ink, --muted, --faint, --rule, --accent + fonts) and pulls cyan via inline var(--mh-blue, #c3f0ff) fallbacks — so the FULL palette's reference implementations are the three tools, not the CV.

gamePalette._note: autofac.html is an intentionally SEPARATE dark 'game' sub-brand (the DrawScreen aesthetic). Do NOT apply it to documents/tools; listed here so builders know it is deliberate, not drift.
